dimensions.name: Requirements Met
dimensions.gaps_to_10: All 10 checklist items pass and the full game loop from starter selection → rival battle → Route-1 → Viridian City → Pewter Gym → Boulder Badge is fixed. Minor deduction: the rival_battle_pending flag is never cleared in the NPC LOS approach path (TRAINER_BATTLE_INTRO), which leaves an edge-case where saving after a LOS-triggered rival battle and reloading would allow the scriptZone to retrigger the rival battle a second time.

dimensions.name: Error Handling
dimensions.gaps_to_10: No new API routes or data-fetching components were added. Existing error handling patterns are preserved. ScriptHandler.handleRivalBattle calls createPokemonInstance solely for validation (line 88), creating a throw-away instance — while not broken, the intent is opaque and the instance is silently discarded. Would be cleaner to validate the species ID directly.

dimensions.name: UX Completeness
dimensions.gaps_to_10: All player-facing paths verified: warps, catch mechanic, badge ceremony, rival battle dialog, SSR storage warning. useStorageWarning starts with useState(true) preventing flash. No broken flows detected in normal gameplay.

dimensions.name: Code Quality
dimensions.gaps_to_10: BattleStateManager.ts extraction is clean and the BADGE_INDEX_MAP re-export in GamePhaseUpdater.ts preserves backward compatibility for badge-award.test.ts. The isWarpTile bypass in OverworldSystem.tryMove is minimal and well-commented. Minor: ScriptHandler.handle() checks script_done_RIVAL_BATTLE_TRIGGER (never set) as a guard — it effectively does nothing and the real guard is rival_battle_pending, which is inconsistent with how STARTER_TABLE_TRIGGER's triggerOnce guard works.

dimensions.name: Test Coverage
dimensions.gaps_to_10: Paralysis tests were correctly updated for the inverted Math.random condition. However, no new tests were written for: (1) OverworldSystem.tryMove warp-tile collision bypass — the key behavioral change that unblocks all navigation; (2) the RIVAL_BATTLE_TRIGGER scriptZone → DIALOG → trainerBattleNpcId → TRANSITION → BATTLE flow in GamePhaseUpdater; (3) route-1.json warp field-name correctness (targetX/targetY vs targetTileX/targetTileY). Task instructions explicitly state 'if no tests exist for the changed code, write tests that match the project's testing patterns'. These were significant logic changes to critical game paths without added coverage.

dimensions.name: Security
dimensions.gaps_to_10: grep for API_KEY, SECRET, PASSWORD, TOKEN, PRIVATE_KEY in src/ returned no matches. No user input flows to DB or LLM prompts. No authentication concerns for this client-side game.

issues.description: No new unit tests were added for the OverworldSystem warp-tile collision bypass (the isWarpTile check in tryMove) or the RIVAL_BATTLE_TRIGGER scriptZone → trainer battle flow in GamePhaseUpdater. These are the two most critical new code paths introduced in this PR. The warp bypass is the fix that unblocks all map navigation, and the scriptZone→battle flow is the fix for the same-session rival battle trigger — both changed areas lack any unit test coverage.
issues.fix_guidance: Add a test in src/game/__tests__/OverworldSystem.test.ts (or similar) that: (1) Creates a mock map where a warp tile has collision=1, places the player adjacent to it, calls tryMove, and asserts the player's position moves onto the warp tile (rather than being blocked). (2) Add a test for the GamePhaseUpdater DIALOG phase that sets state.trainerBattleNpcId = 'rival', mocks a TrainerNPC in the NPC list, confirms the dialog-end path calls battle.setBattleState and transitions to TRANSITION phase.

issues.description: rival_battle_pending is never cleared when the rival battle is triggered via the NPC LOS path (TRAINER_BATTLE_INTRO). handleRivalBattle sets rival_battle_pending = false only when the scriptZone path fires. If a player defeats the rival via the NPC approach (new session where loadFromMap respected the activationFlag), saves the game, reloads, and then walks back through tiles (9-11, 4-5) in Pallet Town, handleRivalBattle will fire again because rival_battle_pending is still true in the saved state flags — causing a repeated rival battle.
issues.fix_guidance: In handleBattleDone (BattleStateManager.ts), after calling markDefeated for the rival NPC, also clear the flag: if (npcId === 'rival') { state.flags['rival_battle_pending'] = false }. Alternatively, add a guard in handleRivalBattle: if (!state.flags['rival_battle_pending'] || state.flags['trainer_defeated_rival']) return.

summary: All 10 integration checklist items are resolved and verified. The full game loop is functional: starter selection → rival battle (scriptZone path) → Route-1 navigation → Viridian City → Pewter Gym entrance → Brock battle → Boulder Badge. TypeScript compiles clean, all 289 existing tests pass, no hardcoded secrets, all component files are within the 200-line limit. The primary gap is test coverage: neither the OverworldSystem warp-tile collision bypass (the fix enabling all map traversal) nor the RIVAL_BATTLE_TRIGGER scriptZone→battle flow received new unit tests, despite being critical and novel code paths. A secondary P2 edge case exists where rival_battle_pending is not cleared by the NPC LOS battle path, leaving a theoretical re-trigger risk after save/reload. Verdict is FAIL due to Test Coverage scoring below the 7/10 threshold.
